title: grassy gooey tunisian shiso-leaf tagine
description: Rich, comforting and potentially hangover-curing, this traditional tunisian dish features the pungent flavor of shiso leaf and a gooey beurre monte. Serve over couscous or with crusty bread for a satisfying meal.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large clay tagine over medium heat.
Add the onion and saut7; until it is translucent and slightly caramelized.
Add the garlic, cumin, coriander, cinnamon, and turmeric; saut7; for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the shiso leaves and cook, stirring occasionally, until they are wilted and fragrant.
Add the chicken broth and bring to a simmer.
Add the beurre monte and stir until it is melted and the sauce is smooth.
Add the dried apricots and cook, stirring occasionally, until they are tender and the sauce has thickened.
Simmer the tagine, uncovered, for 20-25 minutes, until the sauce has thickened and the flavors have melded together.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with additional shiso leaves and beurre monte, if desired.
Let the tagine cool to room temperature before refrigerating or freezing it, if desired.
When reheating the tagine, add a little water if the sauce has thickened too much, or a little beurre monte if it has broken.
Serve the tagine over couscous or with crusty bread, and enjoy!
